steps.title: Resuspend cell pellet in lysis buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend cell pellet in lysis buffer: 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 1 mM EDTA, 0.25 mg/mL lysozyme (use ~5–10 mL buffer per g wet cell pellet). Incubate briefly on ice (5–15 min) to allow lysozyme action.

steps.title: Lyse cells using a French press (≥1000 psi) or equivalent
steps.instruction: Lyse cells using a French press (≥1000 psi) or equivalent. Keep lysate cold.

steps.title: Add streptomycin sulfate to 0
steps.instruction: Add streptomycin sulfate to 0.1% (w/v) to precipitate nucleic acids; mix and incubate briefly (a few minutes).

steps.title: Clarify lysate by centrifugation (e
steps.instruction: Clarify lysate by centrifugation (e.g., 15,000 × g, 20–30 min, 4°C). Collect supernatant.

steps.title: Perform ammonium sulfate precipitation sequentially at 4°C
steps.instruction: Perform ammonium sulfate precipitation sequentially at 4°C: first to 30% saturation, remove pellet (optional), then increase to 50% saturation and collect pellet containing α-syn. (Adjust volumes/salt amounts per standard ammonium sulfate tables.)

steps.title: Resuspend the 50% pellet in 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7
steps.instruction: Resuspend the 50% pellet in 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4.

steps.title: Heat the resuspended sample at 95°C...
steps.instruction: Heat the resuspended sample at 95°C for 15 min (boiling step) to denature many contaminants. Cool on ice.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 13,500 × g, 20...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 13,500 × g, 20 min, 4°C to pellet denatured proteins. Retain the supernatant (contains soluble α-syn).

steps.title: Size exclusion chromatography (SEC)
steps.instruction: Size exclusion chromatography (SEC): Load supernatant on HiLoad 16/600 Superdex 200 pg, elute in 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4. Collect fractions.

steps.title: Anion exchange
steps.instruction: Anion exchange: Pool α-syn-enriched SEC fractions (monitor by SDS-PAGE), then run on HiPrep Q HP 16/10 or DEAE in 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4 + 1 mM EDTA. Elute with a linear NaCl gradient from 25 mM → 1 M NaCl. Collect fractions.

steps.title: Analyze fractions by SDS-PAGE (Coomassie) and...
steps.instruction: Analyze fractions by SDS-PAGE (Coomassie) and pool those with α-syn (target purity ≈ 95%).

steps.title: Dialyze pooled fractions against PBS (pH 7
steps.instruction: Dialyze pooled fractions against PBS (pH 7.4) to exchange buffer.

steps.title: Remove endotoxin using Pierce high-capacity endotoxin...
steps.instruction: Remove endotoxin using Pierce high-capacity endotoxin removal resin per manufacturer’s instructions; aim for ≤0.018 EU/µg protein (verify with LAL or equivalent endotoxin assay).

steps.title: Determine protein concentration (see QC below)
steps.instruction: Determine protein concentration (see QC below). Aliquot and store at −80°C.

steps.title: Expected
steps.instruction: Expected: final purity ~95% (SDS-PAGE).

steps.title: Filter monomeric α-syn solution (prepare at...
steps.instruction: Filter monomeric α-syn solution (prepare at 5 mg/mL) through a 0.22 µm filter into sterile 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes. Example: 500 µL of 5 mg/mL.

steps.title: Incubate at 37°C with continuous agitation...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37°C with continuous agitation at 1,000 rpm in a Thermomixer for 7 days to form fibrils. Use sterile conditions.

steps.title: After 7 days, pellet fibrils by...
steps.instruction: After 7 days, pellet fibrils by centrifugation at 13,000 × g, 10 min. Carefully remove and discard supernatant. Resuspend pellet in 250 µL DPBS.

steps.title: Take a small aliquot (5 µL)...
steps.instruction: Take a small aliquot (5 µL) of fibril suspension and incubate with 8 M guanidine-HCl (5 µL) at 22°C for 1 h to dissociate fibrils into monomers for concentration measurement.

steps.title: Measure A₂₈₀ on a Nanodrop / spectrophotometer
steps.instruction: Measure A₂₈₀ on a Nanodrop / spectrophotometer. Use extinction coefficient ε₂₈₀ = 7450 M⁻¹ cm⁻¹ to calculate molar concentration (A / ε), then convert to mg/mL using the molecular weight (use literature value for mouse α-syn).

steps.title: Formula
steps.instruction: Formula: Molarity (M) = A₂₈₀ / 7450. Mass concentration (g/L) = M × MW. Convert to mg/mL as needed. Note: Adjust for guanidine-HCl dilution step.

steps.title: Store fibrils as 25 µL aliquots...
steps.instruction: Store fibrils as 25 µL aliquots at 5 mg/mL at −80°C.

steps.title: Thaw a 25 µL aliquot on...
steps.instruction: Thaw a 25 µL aliquot on ice when ready to use. Transfer to an ethanol-sterilized sonication tube (e.g., Active Motif #53071).

steps.title: Sonicate in a cup-horn sonicator (Qsonica...
steps.instruction: Sonicate in a cup-horn sonicator (Qsonica q700) at 30% amplitude (~100 W/s) using a cycle of 3 s ON / 2 s OFF, for a total ‘on’ time of 15 min (i.e., multiple cycles). Maintain water bath temperature between 5–15°C (use ice or chilled circulating bath).

steps.title: After sonication, keep samples at room...
steps.instruction: After sonication, keep samples at room temperature and use promptly for injections or downstream assays.

steps.title: Harvest cells by centrifugation (e
steps.instruction: Harvest cells by centrifugation (e.g., 5,000 × g, 10 min, 4°C). Proceed immediately to lysis or freeze pellets at −80°C.

steps.title: Induce expression with IPTG to a...
steps.instruction: Induce expression with IPTG to a final concentration of 1 mM. Continue incubation at 37°C for 4 h.

steps.title: Inoculate BL21(DE3) + pT7-7 (mouse α-syn)...
steps.instruction: Inoculate BL21(DE3) + pT7-7 (mouse α-syn) into LB + ampicillin (100 µg/mL). Grow at 37°C with shaking until OD₆₀₀ = 0.5–0.6.
